description:
You are an energy economist at the OECD estimating the price and income elasticity of gasoline demand to inform environmental policy using a panel dataset of 18 OECD countries over 19 years. Perform a panel data econometric analysis using the plm package in R on the Gasoline dataset. Fit three log-log models for gasoline consumption per car (lgaspcar) predicted by real income (lincomep) and real gasoline price (lrpmg): Pooled OLS, Fixed Effects (Within), and Random Effects. Conduct a Hausman Test to compare the Fixed and Random Effects models.

Save the following outputs to /home/ga/RProjects/output/:
- model_comparison.csv: A table with columns model ('pooled', 'fixed', 'random'), price_elasticity (coefficient for lrpmg), income_elasticity (coefficient for lincomep), and adj_r_squared.
- hausman_result.txt: A text file containing the p-value of the Hausman test and your conclusion regarding which estimator is consistent.
- heterogeneity_plot.png: A visualization showing the heterogeneity in gasoline consumption across countries (such as a boxplot of lgaspcar by country).
- Save the R script as /home/ga/RProjects/gasoline_analysis.R.

Note: The dataset uses logarithmic variables, so coefficients are directly interpreted as elasticities.
